collectiondata.overview:
Best Tour Packages for Kerala from India – Explore God’s Own Country

Looking for the best tour packages for Kerala from India? Discover the tranquil backwaters, misty hill stations, and golden beaches of Kerala — fondly known as “God’s Own Country.” Whether you’re planning a romantic honeymoon package, a rejuvenating wellness retreat, or a fun-filled family vacation, our expertly curated Tour Packages For Kerala cater to every traveler’s dream.


Why Choose Kerala for Your Next Holiday?

Serene Backwaters and Houseboat Experiences
Cruise through the tranquil backwaters of Alleppey and Kumarakom in a traditional houseboat. The gentle lapping of water and lush palm-fringed shores make this a must-do in all Kerala tour packages from Hyderabad, Mumbai, or Delhi.

Lush Hill Stations and Tea Plantations
Escape to Munnar’s rolling hills and endless tea estates. Cool weather, fragrant spice plantations, and panoramic views make hill stations a top pick for kerala tour packages for family and honeymoon packages alike.

Beaches, Wildlife, Ayurveda, and Cultural Delights
Kerala offers a complete travel experience. Relax on the beaches of Kovalam, explore the Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary in Thekkady, and indulge in Ayurveda massages or watch Kathakali dance performances as part of our affordable Kerala backwaters tour packages.

Top Kerala Tour Packages from India​

5 Days Kerala Tour Package from India – Backwaters and Beaches
Ideal for short getaways, this package includes Munnar, Thekkady, and Alleppey. It’s a top choice for travelers booking Kerala tour packages from Bangalore, Pune, or Mumbai.

Kerala Honeymoon Packages from India – Romantic Escapes
Our budget-friendly Kerala honeymoon packages feature romantic houseboat stays, candlelight dinners, hill station visits, and private sightseeing. A perfect start to your journey as a couple, especially for those seeking honeymoon packages from Kolkata with scenic views and cultural experiences.

Budget Kerala Tour Packages for Indian Families
Designed for comfort and fun, these Kerala tour packages for family include family-friendly accommodations, safe transport, and guided tours to top attractions. For the best value, consider packages from a travel agency in Kolkata near me that offer verified group deals.

Customizable Kerala Travel Packages from Major Indian Cities
Whether you're searching for Kerala tour packages from Hyderabad, Kerala tour packages from Delhi, or Kerala tour packages from Pune, we offer custom plans that suit your location, preferences, and budget.

Must-Visit Destinations Covered in Kerala Tour Packages

Munnar – Misty Hills and Tea Estates
With sprawling tea gardens, waterfalls, and national parks, Munnar is a refreshing escape from city life. Perfect for couples and families alike.

Alleppey – Houseboat Cruises in the Backwaters
A highlight of most Kerala tour packages with price transparency, Alleppey offers houseboat day cruises or overnight stays with delicious Kerala cuisine onboard.

Thekkady – Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary
Ideal for nature lovers, Thekkady offers bamboo rafting, jungle walks, and wildlife spotting. It’s a great addition to affordable Kerala backwaters tour packages.

Kochi – Heritage, Culture, and Cuisine
Visit Fort Kochi, Jew Town, and Chinese fishing nets. Don’t miss the spice markets or a live Kathakali performance during your trip.

Kovalam and Varkala – Sun-Kissed Beaches
Unwind on the palm-lined beaches of Kovalam and Varkala. Popular for surfing, ayurvedic retreats, and cliffside cafes, these are top picks in Kerala tour packages from Mumbai and Bangalore.

What’s Included in the Best Kerala Tour Packages from India?

Hotel Stays with Daily Breakfast
All our Tour Packages For Kerala include clean, comfortable stays ranging from 3-star to luxury resorts with daily breakfast options. Family packages offer larger rooms and child-friendly amenities.

Airport Transfers and Local Transportation
Enjoy hassle-free travel with included airport pickups, intercity transfers, and private cabs for sightseeing.

Guided Sightseeing and Optional Experiences (Ayurveda, Kathakali)

Add enriching experiences like:

1. Authentic Ayurvedic massages
2. Kathakali and Kalaripayattu shows
3. Tea estate visits and spice garden tours
(Availability may depend on season and local scheduling.)

​Best Time to Visit Kerala from India

Winter (October to February) – Peak Tourist Season
Pleasant weather makes this the best time for exploring beaches, backwaters, and hill stations. It’s the most popular season for honeymoon packages and families.

Monsoon (June to September) – Ideal for Ayurveda Treatments
This is the best time for wellness retreats, as the rainy season enhances the effects of traditional Ayurvedic therapies. Fewer tourists also mean better deals on Kerala tour packages with price discounts.

Travel Tips for Every Season

Winter: Book at least 30–60 days in advance
Monsoon: Carry quick-dry clothing and mosquito repellent
Summer: Stay hydrated and plan hill station stays to escape the heat

Frequently Asked Questions About Kerala Tour Packages from India

What is the ideal duration for a Kerala tour from India?
A 5 to 7-day itinerary covers the top highlights — Munnar, Thekkady, and Alleppey. For a more relaxed pace or added beach time, opt for 8 to 10 days.

Are Kerala packages suitable for senior citizens and kids?
Yes. Our Kerala tour packages for family include gentle sightseeing, safe accommodations, and vehicle support for senior travelers and young children.

Can Kerala packages be customized based on preferences or budget?
Absolutely. You can select destination combos, upgrade accommodations, include specific experiences like Ayurveda treatments, or adjust your itinerary based on your travel dates and budget.

Do you offer affordable Kerala tour packages for couples?
Yes. Our affordable Kerala tour packages for couples include romantic hill stations, private transport, candlelight dinners, and exclusive houseboat stays — all tailored within a modest budget.

Are there budget-friendly Kerala honeymoon packages?
Yes. We offer budget-friendly Kerala honeymoon packages that include cozy resorts, shikara rides, scenic drives, and cultural shows — all under ₹25,000 depending on duration and season.

Do you provide affordable Kerala backwaters tour packages?
Yes. Our affordable Kerala backwaters tour packages include day or overnight houseboat cruises, backwater village visits, and authentic Kerala meals — perfect for couples or families.

How to Book the Best Tour Packages for Kerala from India

Recommended Travel Agencies and Online Booking Sites
Choose licensed travel agents with Kerala Tourism approval or trusted online platforms offering verified reviews, transparent pricing, and flexible customization options. TravelXploria, one of the best tours and travel agency in India, is also ranked among the best domestic tour operators in India and best tour and travel company in India for its quality service.

What to Check Before Confirming Your Kerala Trip

1. Hotel category and meal plan
2. Sightseeing inclusions and transfers
3. Cancellation/refund policies
4. Kerala tour packages with price breakdowns and optional upgrades

Documents and Packing List for Kerala Travel

1. Valid government-issued ID (Aadhar, PAN, or Passport)
2. Lightweight cotton clothes, sunblock, and sunglasses
3. Rain gear (June–September)
4. Travel chargers, footwear for walking trails, and personal medications

• ​Report to Willington Island, Cochin, between 9 AM to 10:30 AM for boarding; set sail after lunch at 1 PM to Minicoy.

  • Explore Minicoy Island with a visit to the Tuna Canning Factory and Museum (optional).
  • Enjoy water sports at the resort, including scuba diving or snorkeling in Minicoy's coral reefs.

  • After lunch, board the ship for the journey back to Cochin, departing at 6 PM.
  • Arrive in Cochin by 10 AM; disembark after breakfast. If traveling, ensure bookings are made after 2 PM.​

packages.overview:
Munnar

Hill station known for tea gardens and cool climate

  • Tea Plantations: Endless stretches of lush green tea estates.

  • Eravikulam National Park: Home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr and Neelakurinji blooms.

  • Mattupetty Dam & Echo Point: Scenic picnic spots with boating and nature views.

  • Kundala Lake: Serene lake surrounded by rolling hills and pedal boating options.

  • Tea Museum: Insight into Munnar’s colonial tea heritage.

Alleppey

Famous for its tranquil backwaters and houseboat stays

  • Houseboat Cruise: Sail through the scenic backwaters, passing villages and paddy fields.

  • Alleppey Beach: Golden sands and a peaceful seaside vibe.

  • Backwater Canals: Ideal for a peaceful canoe ride or photo session.

  • Traditional Kerala Cuisine: Enjoy authentic meals served on a houseboat.

Varkala

Coastal town known for cliffside views and spiritual vibes

  • Varkala Cliff & Beach: Unique red cliffs overlooking the Arabian Sea, great for sunsets.

  • Janardanaswamy Temple: Ancient temple with spiritual significance.

  • Papanasam Beach: Believed to wash away sins – a peaceful spot for reflection.

  • Ayurvedic Spas: Rejuvenating massages and wellness treatments.

  • Cafes & Shopping: Laid-back cafes with sea views and local handicrafts along the cliff.

Enjoy a scenic 4-hour drive, passing through picturesque valleys and charming villages. En route, stop at the stunning Cheeyappara & Valara Waterfalls, where the cascading waters create a mesmerizing sight. Upon reaching Munnar, check in to your luxury honeymoon resort, offering a perfect blend of comfort and elegance. packages.package_name: Kerala Unplugged From Highlands to Coastlines
packages.overview: Your romantic journey begins with your arrival in Kochi, followed by a scenic drive to Munnar, where you'll enjoy its breathtaking hill views and lush landscapes. Explore the charming town, surrounded by tea plantations, and experience the serene beauty of the hills. Next, head to Alleppey, where a romantic houseboat cruise awaits, offering stunning backwater views. From there, continue your journey to Varkala, a clifftop paradise where you can unwind by the sea. Enjoy romantic moments by the beach before heading to Kanyakumari, the southern tip of India, for a serene and picturesque sunset experience. Your final day marks the end of this unforgettable journey through the heart of Kerala.

packages.overview:
• Kochi (Cochin) – Known as the Queen of the Arabian Sea, Kochi is a cultural melting pot where colonial charm meets coastal vibrance. Tourists are drawn to its historic sites like Fort Kochi, the Chinese Fishing Nets, and the Mattancherry Palace. The Jewish Synagogue and spice markets add to its multicultural appeal. Kochi also serves as the gateway to Kerala’s hill stations and backwaters.
  • Munnar – A dreamy hill station nestled in the Western Ghats, Munnar is famous for its endless tea plantations, cool climate, and misty hills. It's a haven for nature lovers and honeymooners, offering serene spots like Eravikulam National Park, Tea Museum, and Mattupetty Dam. Trekking, wildlife sightings, and scenic drives make Munnar a must-visit destination.
  • Alleppey (Alappuzha) – Often dubbed the Venice of the East, Alleppey is renowned for its tranquil backwaters and iconic houseboat experiences. A cruise through its lush waterways offers a glimpse into rural Kerala life. Tourists also enjoy Alleppey Beach, Ayurvedic wellness, and authentic Kerala cuisine served on board traditional kettuvallams.
  • Kanyakumari – Located at the southernmost tip of India, Kanyakumari is where the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and Indian Ocean meet. It's a spiritual and scenic destination known for the Vivekananda Rock Memorial, Thiruvalluvar Statue, and stunning sunrise/sunset views. Pilgrims, history buffs, and sea lovers alike are captivated by its unique coastal charm.

packages.overview: Your journey begins with your arrival in Kochi, where you'll take a stroll through time, exploring the city’s rich colonial history and vibrant culture. From Kochi, embark on a scenic journey to Munnar, the enchanting land of tea gardens, where lush hills and picturesque landscapes await. In Munnar, you'll explore its natural wonders, including tea plantations, tranquil lakes, and captivating wildlife. After your time in Munnar, you'll return to Kochi, where you can soak in the final moments of the city before your departure, leaving with lasting memories of Kerala’s charm and beauty.

Upon arrival at Cochin International Airport, our friendly Travelxploria representative will ensure a seamless transfer to your hotel, where you can unwind before diving into Kerala’s colonial charm. Begin your journey in Fort Kochi, strolling through its picturesque streets filled with Dutch, Portuguese, and British colonial architecture. Visit St. Francis Church, the oldest European-built church in India, and marvel at the legendary Chinese Fishing Nets silhouetted against the Arabian Sea at sunset. Explore the Mattancherry Palace (Dutch Palace) with its exquisite murals and artifacts, followed by a visit to Jew Town & Paradesi Synagogue, where the spice-scented lanes tell tales of Kerala’s historic Jewish community. As the evening unfolds, immerse yourself in the grandeur of Kathakali, Kerala’s iconic dance-drama, known for its elaborate costumes and expressive storytelling. Witness the intricate makeup process before enjoying a sumptuous seafood dinner at a heritage restaurant. Retire for the night in the comfort of your hotel, gearing up for the adventures ahead.

After a delightful breakfast, set out for Eravikulam National Park, a UNESCO-listed sanctuary known for its breathtaking landscapes and the rare Nilgiri Tahr. Wander through rolling meadows and misty hills, and if visiting in 2030, witness the mesmerizing bloom of Neelakurinji flowers, which paint the hills in a sea of blue once every 12 years. As the day winds down, savor a delicious sunset dinner at your scenic hilltop retreat before unwinding for the night. Overnight stay in Munnar.

packages.overview: Your journey begins with your arrival in Kochi, the gateway to Kerala's vibrant culture, where you'll explore its rich heritage, historic sites, and bustling markets. From Kochi, travel to Munnar, where the magic of lush tea plantations and misty hills awaits, offering a serene escape into nature. A scenic drive takes you to Thekkady, where you'll immerse yourself in the wild beauty of the Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary, surrounded by lush forests and diverse wildlife. After Thekkady, you'll arrive in Alleppey, known for its tranquil backwaters, where you can unwind on a peaceful houseboat cruise. Your next stop is Varkala, a coastal gem offering beachside serenity, perfect for relaxation and enjoying the sea breeze. Finally, your Kerala adventure concludes with a departure, leaving you with unforgettable memories of this enchanting destination.

After a delightful breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will ensure a smooth and comfortable transfer to Munnar, Kerala’s picturesque hill station. As you embark on this scenic 4-hour drive, enjoy mesmerizing views of rolling tea gardens and misty mountains. En route, pause to admire the Cheeyappara & Valara Waterfalls, capturing stunning cascades amidst lush greenery. Indulge in a fragrant spice plantation tour, where the scents of cardamom, vanilla, and pepper fill the air. packages.overview: Your adventure begins with a warm welcome to Kochi, the Gateway to Kerala, where you'll explore the city’s vibrant markets, colonial architecture, and rich cultural heritage. From Kochi, journey to Munnar, where you'll be captivated by its scenic waterfalls and bustling local markets. In Munnar, discover the natural wonders of lush tea plantations and explore its wildlife in serene surroundings. Next, drift through the tranquil backwaters of Alleppey on a serene houseboat, immersing yourself in the beauty of Kerala’s waterways. Your journey continues with a visit to Kovalam Beach, where you can experience sunset serenity and unwind by the shore. Enjoy thrilling beach adventures and explore the coastal landscape before bidding farewell to Kerala’s enchanting beauty, taking home unforgettable memories of this tropical paradise.

Begin with a visit to the serene Mattupetty Lake and Dam, followed by a tour of the Tea Museum, where you can learn about Munnar’s rich tea heritage. Next, head to Kundale Lake, a picturesque spot perfect for relaxation. After lunch, explore the Eravikulam Wildlife Sanctuary, the last refuge of the endangered Nilgiri Tahr goat. If time permits, visit the Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary and admire the grandeur of Anamudi Hill, South India’s highest peak. Return to your hotel, enjoy a delicious dinner, and retire for the night.

packages.overview:
• Kerala: A paradise of lush greenery, tranquil backwaters, hill stations, and rich wildlife, offering a serene escape.
  • Munnar: A picturesque hill station known for its sprawling tea plantations, Eravikulam National Park, Mattupetty Dam, and breathtaking views.
  • Thekkady: Famous for Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary, offering boat safaris, spice plantations, and abundant wildlife.
  • Alleppey: The “Venice of the East,” renowned for its tranquil backwaters and houseboat cruises through scenic landscapes.

This scenic journey sets the stage for the natural beauty and tranquility of Munnar that awaits.

After a good night’s sleep and a scrumptious breakfast at the hotel, embark on a full-day exploration of Munnar and its stunning surroundings. Start with a visit to local markets in Munnar, perfect for souvenir hunting or window shopping. Then, head out to some of Munnar's most breathtaking sights, including Mattupetty Lake and Dam, Kundale Lake, Echo Point, Rose Garden, Photo Point, and the Blossom Gardens. If you're a tea lover, a visit to the Tea Museum set up by Tata Tea is highly recommended. Continue your tour with stops at Pallivasal/Pothamedu and Rajamalai, where the views are simply mesmerizing. After lunch, visit the Eravikulam Wildlife Sanctuary, the last refuge of the Nilgiri Tahr goat, and take in the diverse flora and fauna. After a day of exploring, return to your hotel for a delicious Kerala-style dinner (or your preferred cuisine) and retire for the night, resting for the next day's adventure.

After an early breakfast, set out for Thekkady, the wildlife hub and spice capital of Kerala, located 110 km away. The scenic drive takes around 4 hours, passing through the lush green hills and valleys of the Western Ghats, offering breathtaking views. Along the way, keep an eye out for wild animals in their natural habitat, and make a stop to visit the spice gardens that Thekkady is famous for. If time allows, indulge in a holistic Ayurveda massage to rejuvenate and relax.

After a delicious lunch, head to Periyar Lake and Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary. A jeep safari inside Periyar National Park is highly recommended (tickets available with forest guides), offering a close look at the rich biodiversity. As evening falls, enjoy a boat ride on Periyar Lake, where you can spot more wild animals along the shore. Return to your nearby hotel for a peaceful night’s rest after an adventurous day.

After an early Kerala-style breakfast, head out to Alleppey, famously known as the Venice of the East, with a seamless transfer by hired bus or taxi. Once you arrive, immerse yourself in the famous backwater channels, where houseboats with modern amenities glide through serene waters, offering a unique way to experience Kerala’s natural beauty. If you can afford, spend the night aboard a luxurious houseboat, where you can witness ancient Chinese fishing nets at work, pass by coconut groves, plantations, and temples, and indulge in the picturesque surroundings. Alternatively, stay in a comfortable hotel, enjoy a boat ride through the backwaters, and spend the rest of the day relaxing. In the evening, treat yourself to a delicious Kerala-style dinner, then retire for the night, cherishing the tranquil beauty of Alleppey.

packages.overview: Your adventure begins with a warm welcome to the "Niagara of India," Athirappilly, where you’ll marvel at the spectacular waterfalls, often referred to as the region’s crown jewel. Explore the natural marvels of Athirappilly, from cascading falls to lush greenery, before continuing your journey to Munnar. In Munnar, embark on a local exploration of its serene landscapes, including vast tea plantations, cool misty hills, and fascinating wildlife. Discover the tranquil beauty of Munnar, where tea, wildlife, and peaceful surroundings create the perfect retreat. Next, drift through the enchanting backwaters of Alleppey on a serene houseboat cruise, immersing yourself in the beauty of Kerala’s waterways. Afterward, experience the sunset bliss at Kovalam Beach, relaxing by the shore as the sun sets over the Arabian Sea. Enjoy thrilling beach adventures and soak in Trivandrum's cultural charm, visiting its temples, palaces, and museums. Finally, bid farewell to Kerala, taking with you cherished memories of its natural wonders, rich culture, and serene beauty.

packages.overview:
Welcome to Kerala, a land where nature whispers in lush green hills, backwaters glide through coconut groves, and beaches stretch endlessly along the Arabian Sea. Fondly known as “God’s Own Country”, Kerala is a tropical paradise that offers a perfect blend of tranquility, adventure, culture, and luxury.

From the serene backwaters of Alleppey and Kumarakom, the misty tea plantations of Munnar, and the wildlife-rich jungles of Thekkady, to the pristine beaches of Varkala and Kovalam, Kerala is a dream come true for every traveler. Experience rejuvenation with authentic Ayurvedic therapies, indulge in vibrant cultural performances like Kathakali and Theyyam, and savor the mouth-watering Kerala cuisine.

Whether you're planning a honeymoon getaway, a family vacation, or a solo escape into nature, Kerala promises unforgettable memories. Travel with us and explore Kerala through expertly crafted itineraries that include luxury stays, guided tours, private transfers, and personalized experiences.

packages.overview: Your journey begins with your arrival in Kochi, where you'll step into the colonial charm of the city, exploring its historic forts and vibrant markets. Afterward, you'll visit Cherai Beach and enjoy a traditional Kathakali dance performance, immersing yourself in Kerala's rich culture. A scenic drive takes you to Thekkady, where you'll experience the wild beauty of the Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary, along with a mix of adventure and cultural activities. From Thekkady, head to Kumarakom, where you can unwind in the serene backwaters and visit the Kumarakom Bird Sanctuary. Next, you'll explore Alleppey, often referred to as the "Venice of the East," as you cruise through its tranquil backwaters. Afterward, journey south to Kanyakumari, the southernmost tip of India, where you can witness a breathtaking sunrise. Spend your leisure day enjoying the natural beauty before your departure from Trivandrum, concluding this unforgettable Kerala adventure.

packages.package_name: Whispers of Kerala A Timeless Tropical Trail
packages.overview:
• Wayanad – Nestled in the lush Western Ghats, Wayanad is a pristine hill station known for its dense forests, waterfalls, and wildlife. It’s a paradise for nature lovers and adventure seekers, offering attractions like Edakkal Caves, Banasura Sagar Dam, and Wayanad Wildlife Sanctuary. The cool climate, spice plantations, and tribal culture make it a unique retreat.
  • Thekkady – Home to the famous Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary, Thekkady is a green haven where nature meets wildlife. Tourists can enjoy a boat ride on Periyar Lake, spot elephants and exotic birds, and explore spice plantations. Thekkady is also known for eco-tourism, bamboo rafting, trekking trails, and traditional martial arts shows.
  • Varkala – A coastal gem, Varkala is known for its dramatic red cliffs overlooking the Arabian Sea. The Papanasam Beach, believed to have sacred cleansing powers, draws spiritual travelers, while the cliffside cafés and Ayurvedic spas attract those seeking relaxation. Varkala offers a perfect blend of peace, natural beauty, and bohemian vibes.

packages.overview: Your journey begins with a visit to Kanyakumari, the southernmost tip of India, where you can marvel at the stunning confluence of the Arabian Sea, the Bay of Bengal, and the Indian Ocean. Next, unwind on the iconic beaches of Kovalam, enjoying the peaceful atmosphere and soothing coastal views. In Trivandrum, immerse yourself in the local culture by exploring its historic temples, majestic palaces, and intriguing museums. Your Kerala adventure comes to a close with a fond farewell to Kovalam, leaving you with lasting memories of its natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Your trip begins with an early arrival at Trivandrum airport or railway station, where you'll be greeted by our Travelxploria representative for a seamless transfer. After some refreshments, embark on a scenic drive to Kanyakumari, located 102 km away, the southernmost tip of mainland India. Upon arrival, check in at your hotel and unwind.

In the afternoon, set out to explore Kanyakumari’s iconic landmarks, starting with the 1000-year-old Konerishwarar Temple and the Kumari Amman Temple. Take a boat ride to the Vivekananda Rock Memorial, and marvel at the Thiruvalluvar Statue and the historical Vattakottai Fort. Don't miss visiting the Lady of Ransom Church. In the evening, take time for some window shopping in the local markets.

Return to your hotel after an enriching day of sightseeing and enjoy a delightful dinner before retiring for the night.

Get up early and after a scrumptious breakfast, head out to the beach to enjoy exciting water sports and other fun activities along the coastline. After a fulfilling lunch, take a scenic drive to Trivandrum to explore its rich history and culture.

Begin your tour with a visit to the Lord Vishnu Temple (please note the dress code must be followed). Next, head to Kuthiramalika Palace to admire its architectural beauty and historical significance. If time permits, stop by the Trivandrum Zoo for a glimpse of local wildlife.

After lunch, visit the iconic Sri Anantha Padmanabha Swamy Temple, followed by the Napier Museum and the Sri Chitra Art Gallery to explore Kerala's art and culture.

Return to the hotel in the evening, unwind, and enjoy a delectable dinner before retiring for the night, reflecting on the day’s exploration.

packages.overview:
• Arrive Alleppey: Reach the jetty and board your traditional houseboat.

  • Backwater Cruise: Glide through Vembanad Lake and maze‑like canals.

  • Onboard Dining: Savor authentic Kerala cuisine as you drift.

  • Village Stop: Pause for a canoe ride and glimpse daily life in waterside hamlets.

  • Sunset View: Watch the sun dip over the tranquil backwaters.

  • Overnight Afloat: Sleep aboard under a canopy of stars.

  • Morning Walk: Disembark for a stroll along the paddy‑lined shores and visit a coir‑making unit.

  • Depart Alleppey: Return to the jetty for onward travel.

overview:
Best Tour Packages for Kerala from India – Explore God’s Own Country

Looking for the best tour packages for Kerala from India? Discover the tranquil backwaters, misty hill stations, and golden beaches of Kerala — fondly known as “God’s Own Country.” Whether you’re planning a romantic honeymoon package, a rejuvenating wellness retreat, or a fun-filled family vacation, our expertly curated Tour Packages For Kerala cater to every traveler’s dream.


Why Choose Kerala for Your Next Holiday?

Serene Backwaters and Houseboat Experiences
Cruise through the tranquil backwaters of Alleppey and Kumarakom in a traditional houseboat. The gentle lapping of water and lush palm-fringed shores make this a must-do in all Kerala tour packages from Hyderabad, Mumbai, or Delhi.

Lush Hill Stations and Tea Plantations
Escape to Munnar’s rolling hills and endless tea estates. Cool weather, fragrant spice plantations, and panoramic views make hill stations a top pick for kerala tour packages for family and honeymoon packages alike.

Beaches, Wildlife, Ayurveda, and Cultural Delights
Kerala offers a complete travel experience. Relax on the beaches of Kovalam, explore the Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary in Thekkady, and indulge in Ayurveda massages or watch Kathakali dance performances as part of our affordable Kerala backwaters tour packages.

Top Kerala Tour Packages from India​

5 Days Kerala Tour Package from India – Backwaters and Beaches
Ideal for short getaways, this package includes Munnar, Thekkady, and Alleppey. It’s a top choice for travelers booking Kerala tour packages from Bangalore, Pune, or Mumbai.

Kerala Honeymoon Packages from India – Romantic Escapes
Our budget-friendly Kerala honeymoon packages feature romantic houseboat stays, candlelight dinners, hill station visits, and private sightseeing. A perfect start to your journey as a couple, especially for those seeking honeymoon packages from Kolkata with scenic views and cultural experiences.

Budget Kerala Tour Packages for Indian Families
Designed for comfort and fun, these Kerala tour packages for family include family-friendly accommodations, safe transport, and guided tours to top attractions. For the best value, consider packages from a travel agency in Kolkata near me that offer verified group deals.

Customizable Kerala Travel Packages from Major Indian Cities
Whether you're searching for Kerala tour packages from Hyderabad, Kerala tour packages from Delhi, or Kerala tour packages from Pune, we offer custom plans that suit your location, preferences, and budget.

Must-Visit Destinations Covered in Kerala Tour Packages

Munnar – Misty Hills and Tea Estates
With sprawling tea gardens, waterfalls, and national parks, Munnar is a refreshing escape from city life. Perfect for couples and families alike.

Alleppey – Houseboat Cruises in the Backwaters
A highlight of most Kerala tour packages with price transparency, Alleppey offers houseboat day cruises or overnight stays with delicious Kerala cuisine onboard.

Thekkady – Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary
Ideal for nature lovers, Thekkady offers bamboo rafting, jungle walks, and wildlife spotting. It’s a great addition to affordable Kerala backwaters tour packages.

Kochi – Heritage, Culture, and Cuisine
Visit Fort Kochi, Jew Town, and Chinese fishing nets. Don’t miss the spice markets or a live Kathakali performance during your trip.

Kovalam and Varkala – Sun-Kissed Beaches
Unwind on the palm-lined beaches of Kovalam and Varkala. Popular for surfing, ayurvedic retreats, and cliffside cafes, these are top picks in Kerala tour packages from Mumbai and Bangalore.

What’s Included in the Best Kerala Tour Packages from India?

Hotel Stays with Daily Breakfast
All our Tour Packages For Kerala include clean, comfortable stays ranging from 3-star to luxury resorts with daily breakfast options. Family packages offer larger rooms and child-friendly amenities.

Airport Transfers and Local Transportation
Enjoy hassle-free travel with included airport pickups, intercity transfers, and private cabs for sightseeing.

Guided Sightseeing and Optional Experiences (Ayurveda, Kathakali)

Add enriching experiences like:

1. Authentic Ayurvedic massages
2. Kathakali and Kalaripayattu shows
3. Tea estate visits and spice garden tours
(Availability may depend on season and local scheduling.)

​Best Time to Visit Kerala from India

Winter (October to February) – Peak Tourist Season
Pleasant weather makes this the best time for exploring beaches, backwaters, and hill stations. It’s the most popular season for honeymoon packages and families.

Monsoon (June to September) – Ideal for Ayurveda Treatments
This is the best time for wellness retreats, as the rainy season enhances the effects of traditional Ayurvedic therapies. Fewer tourists also mean better deals on Kerala tour packages with price discounts.

Travel Tips for Every Season

Winter: Book at least 30–60 days in advance
Monsoon: Carry quick-dry clothing and mosquito repellent
Summer: Stay hydrated and plan hill station stays to escape the heat

Frequently Asked Questions About Kerala Tour Packages from India

What is the ideal duration for a Kerala tour from India?
A 5 to 7-day itinerary covers the top highlights — Munnar, Thekkady, and Alleppey. For a more relaxed pace or added beach time, opt for 8 to 10 days.

Are Kerala packages suitable for senior citizens and kids?
Yes. Our Kerala tour packages for family include gentle sightseeing, safe accommodations, and vehicle support for senior travelers and young children.

Can Kerala packages be customized based on preferences or budget?
Absolutely. You can select destination combos, upgrade accommodations, include specific experiences like Ayurveda treatments, or adjust your itinerary based on your travel dates and budget.

Do you offer affordable Kerala tour packages for couples?
Yes. Our affordable Kerala tour packages for couples include romantic hill stations, private transport, candlelight dinners, and exclusive houseboat stays — all tailored within a modest budget.

Are there budget-friendly Kerala honeymoon packages?
Yes. We offer budget-friendly Kerala honeymoon packages that include cozy resorts, shikara rides, scenic drives, and cultural shows — all under ₹25,000 depending on duration and season.

Do you provide affordable Kerala backwaters tour packages?
Yes. Our affordable Kerala backwaters tour packages include day or overnight houseboat cruises, backwater village visits, and authentic Kerala meals — perfect for couples or families.

How to Book the Best Tour Packages for Kerala from India

Recommended Travel Agencies and Online Booking Sites
Choose licensed travel agents with Kerala Tourism approval or trusted online platforms offering verified reviews, transparent pricing, and flexible customization options. TravelXploria, one of the best tours and travel agency in India, is also ranked among the best domestic tour operators in India and best tour and travel company in India for its quality service.

What to Check Before Confirming Your Kerala Trip

1. Hotel category and meal plan
2. Sightseeing inclusions and transfers
3. Cancellation/refund policies
4. Kerala tour packages with price breakdowns and optional upgrades

Documents and Packing List for Kerala Travel

1. Valid government-issued ID (Aadhar, PAN, or Passport)
2. Lightweight cotton clothes, sunblock, and sunglasses
3. Rain gear (June–September)
4. Travel chargers, footwear for walking trails, and personal medications
